summary: Verified the react/no-unescaped-entities fix on /app/frontend/src/pages/StudyPlan.jsx line 106. The apostrophe is now encoded as ' in source. Rendered output in browser is a literal 'What's included' (no ' leaks to DOM). No regressions on Study Plan page or on /, /knowledge-base, /career-hub. Zero console errors / page errors.
critical_code_review_comments: Note (informational only, not in scope): repo uses ESLint v9 globally (npx eslint) but project has no eslint.config.js; CRA's internal lint pipeline still works during react-scripts build. Direct CLI lint of a single file from /app/frontend therefore can't be executed without scaffolding a flat-config. Verification was done via runtime render check + source inspection. If main agent wants a CLI-runnable lint, add an eslint.config.js or run via `CI=true yarn build`.
context_for_next_testing_agent: StudyPlan apostrophe fix verified. Heading SPAN textContent = "What's included" (literal apostrophe). All 5 content tiles render (Related KB articles=30, Core questions/Scenario practice/Key concepts/Failure patterns/Mock interview = Coming soon). All 6 stages render. Other routes /, /knowledge-base, /career-hub load cleanly with 0 console errors.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: CRA build runs ESLint with react/no-unescaped-entities rule; a literal apostrophe in JSX text node (`What's included`) triggered the rule and, under CI=true, failed the build. Fix: replaced literal `'` with HTML entity `'`. JSX/HTML decode the entity at render time, so visible text is identical. Verified via DOM inspection: SPAN.innerText === "What's included" and no `'` string present anywhere in document.body.innerText.
